title: centaurea micrantha subsp. herminii
intro: Centaurea micrantha subsp. herminii, also known as Hermin's knapweed or small-flowered knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea micrantha plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This perennial herb is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds, which provide numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Centaurea micrantha subsp. herminii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
